title: C4 Content Creation Coordinator
description: Build the foundational C4 platform: iOS app + Node.js backend on localhost with AI image generation, AI video generation, content project management, basic video assembly, and credit-based usage tracking. No auth, single user, localhost deployment.
close_reason: C4 Content Creation Coordinator - all 8 phases complete

title: Setup: Project Scaffolding
description: Scaffold iOS Xcode project with SPM modules and Node.js backend with Fastify. Install dependencies, configure linting, establish project structure.

title: Initialize Node.js backend with Fastify
description: Create backend/package.json with all deps (fastify, @fastify/swagger, @fastify/websocket, pg, knex, bullmq, ioredis, sharp, pino, openai, @fal-ai/client, @runwayml/sdk, dotenv). Set up project structure.
close_reason: Created backend/package.json with all deps, ran npm install

title: Create iOS Xcode project with SPM modules
description: Create ios/C4/ Xcode project with SPM module structure: CoreKit, ProjectFeature, GenerateFeature, AssemblyFeature, CreditFeature. Configure SwiftUI app entry point.
close_reason: iOS Xcode project created with SPM module structure: CoreKit, ProjectFeature, GenerateFeature, AssemblyFeature, CreditFeature. All Package.swift files configured with swift-tools-version 6.0, TCA dependency, and proper target structure.

title: Create config loader and .env.example
description: Create backend/src/config.js for loading env vars. Create .env.example with all required API keys (OpenAI, fal.ai, Runway, Creatomate, Deepgram, DB, Redis).

title: Foundational: Database, API, Job Queue, Credits
description: Core infrastructure: PostgreSQL schema, Redis+BullMQ, local storage, WebSocket server, credit accounting, Fastify plugins, iOS CoreKit module.

title: Create PostgreSQL schema migration
description: Create backend/src/db/migrations/001_initial.js with tables: projects, assets, notes, credit_accounts, credit_transactions, generation_jobs.

title: Create database plugin (pg pool)
description: Create backend/src/plugins/database.js - Fastify plugin wrapping pg connection pool with decorators.

title: Create Redis + BullMQ plugin
description: Create backend/src/plugins/redis.js - Redis connection and BullMQ queue initialization for generation jobs.

title: Create local storage plugin
description: Create backend/src/plugins/storage.js - Local filesystem storage with path conventions ./storage/assets/{projectId}/{assetId}.{ext}.

title: Create WebSocket plugin
description: Create backend/src/plugins/websocket.js - WebSocket server for real-time progress broadcast to iOS clients.

title: Create credit accounting service
description: Create backend/src/services/credits.js - Balance check, deduct, refund, history. Atomic transactions via PostgreSQL.

title: Create Fastify server entry point
description: Create backend/src/server.js - Register all plugins (db, redis, websocket, storage) and route prefixes. Configure CORS, logging (Pino).

title: Create iOS CoreKit module
description: Create ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/ with: APIClient (URLSession), WebSocketManager, shared models (Project, Asset, Credit, GenerationJob).
close_reason: CoreKit module built with APIClient (URLSession-based, targeting localhost:3000), WebSocketClient (auto-reconnect), and all models: Project, Asset, Credit/CreditTransaction, GenerationJob, Note. All types are Codable, Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable structs with Swift 6 concurrency compliance.

title: Create Knex config
description: Create backend/knexfile.js for localhost PostgreSQL connection settings and migration directory config.

title: US1: AI Image Generation
description: Multi-provider image generation pipeline: iOS prompt UI → backend API → AI providers (OpenAI, FLUX/fal.ai, Nano Banana) → BullMQ job → WebSocket progress → store result. Credit deduction on completion.

title: Create multi-provider image service
description: Create backend/src/services/ai-image.js - Provider abstraction for OpenAI GPT Image 1.5, FLUX via fal.ai, Nano Banana/Imagen. Provider selection by quality tier.
close_reason: Multi-provider image service: OpenAI, FLUX, Nano Banana/Imagen, Grok Imagine with tier-based selection

title: Create image generation BullMQ worker
description: Create backend/src/workers/generation.js - Process image gen jobs, call ai-image service, emit WebSocket progress, store result, deduct credits.

title: Create /api/generate/image route
description: Add POST /api/generate/image to backend/src/routes/generate.js - JSON Schema validation, credit check, job dispatch to BullMQ.
close_reason: POST /api/generate/image route with JSON Schema validation, credit check, asset record, BullMQ dispatch

title: Create iOS image generation UI

title: Create iOS image generation TCA reducer
description: Create ios/.../GenerateFeature/Sources/ImageGenerateReducer.swift - States: idle/generating/progress/complete/error. Effects for API calls and WebSocket.

description: Create ios/.../CoreKit/Sources/WebSocketClient.swift - Connect to backend WS, parse progress messages, dispatch to TCA store.

title: Add Grok Imagine provider to image service
description: Add x.ai Grok Imagine (grok-imagine-image, grok-imagine-image-pro) as a provider in the multi-provider image generation service. Use OpenAI SDK with baseURL https://api.x.ai/v1. Support both standard and pro models. Add XAI_API_KEY to config.
close_reason: Grok Imagine provider added to ai-image.js using openai SDK with x.ai baseURL

title: US2: AI Video Generation
description: Multi-provider video generation pipeline: text-to-video and image-to-video modes via Kling 3.0, Runway Gen-4, Hailuo (fal.ai gateway). Async job processing with WebSocket progress.

title: Create multi-provider video service
description: Create backend/src/services/ai-video.js - Provider abstraction for Kling 3.0/fal.ai, Runway Gen-4, Hailuo, Veo 3.1 Fast. Text-to-video and image-to-video modes.

title: Add video extension UI to iOS GenerateFeature
description: Add video extension capability to the iOS video generation UI. When viewing a generated video clip, show an 'Extend' button. Present a sheet for continuation prompt and target duration. Show progress via WebSocket as extension chains are processed. Preview the extended result with playback.

title: Add video job type to BullMQ worker
description: Extend backend/src/workers/generation.js - Video generation job processing with async polling for provider completion status.

title: Create /api/generate/video route
description: Add POST /api/generate/video to backend/src/routes/generate.js - Support text-to-video and image-to-video with duration, aspect ratio params.

title: Create iOS video generation UI
description: Create ios/.../GenerateFeature/Sources/VideoGenerateView.swift - Mode selector (text/image), duration picker, aspect ratio, quality tier.

title: Create iOS video generation TCA reducer
description: Create ios/.../GenerateFeature/Sources/VideoGenerateReducer.swift - Video gen state with progress tracking and video playback integration.

title: Create video thumbnail utility
description: Create backend/src/services/thumbnails.js - Generate thumbnail images from video files using ffmpeg/sharp for project grid display.

title: Add Grok Imagine provider to video service
description: Add x.ai Grok Imagine Video (grok-imagine-video) as a provider in the multi-provider video generation service. Support text-to-video, image-to-video, and video editing modes. Use OpenAI SDK with baseURL https://api.x.ai/v1. Implement async polling for video completion.

title: Create video extension service with Grok Imagine
description: Build a video extension service that chains Grok Imagine video generations to extend clips. Workflow: take final frame of input video, call /v1/videos/generations with video_url, poll for result, concatenate clips with ffmpeg. Support iterative extensions up to 30 seconds. Handle temporary URL downloads and re-hosting. Add fluent-ffmpeg for concatenation.

title: Create /api/generate/video/extend route
description: Create POST /api/generate/video/extend endpoint. Accepts: assetId (source video), prompt (continuation description), maxDuration (target total length). Validates source video exists and is under 8.7s per segment. Creates BullMQ job for extension pipeline. Returns job ID for WebSocket progress tracking. Deducts credits per second of extension.

description: CRUD for content projects with notes. Asset browsing in grid view, image/video preview, note-taking for ideas. Organizational container for all generated content.

description: Create backend/src/routes/projects.js - POST/GET/PUT/DELETE for projects. JSON Schema validation. List with pagination.

description: Create backend/src/routes/assets.js - GET list by project, GET single, DELETE. Return asset metadata with file URLs.

title: Create notes CRUD routes
description: Create backend/src/routes/notes.js - POST/GET/PUT/DELETE notes per project. Free-form text for capturing content ideas.

description: Create ios/.../ProjectFeature/Sources/AssetPreviewView.swift - Full-screen image viewer and inline video player with AVPlayer.

Update the iOS cost display to show Grok Imagine options.

description: Update generation worker to refund credits when AI generation fails after deduction.

description: Create shared empty state and loading indicator views in CoreKit/SharedViews/ for consistent UX.

description: Create backend/tests/ with tests for generation endpoints, credit flows, project CRUD.

title: Create iOS navigation shell
description: Create tab bar navigation in C4App.swift: Projects tab, Generate tab, Credits tab.

title: Backend generate response doesn't match iOS GenerationJob model
description:
P1: The /api/generate/image response returns { jobId, assetId, status } but the iOS GenerationJob model expects { id, status, progress, provider, type, assetId }. Three problems:

1. Key mismatch: backend sends 'jobId' but iOS struct has 'id' — Codable will fail
2. Missing required fields: 'progress', 'provider', 'type' are non-optional in GenerationJob but not in the backend response
3. Swift Codable auto-synthesis requires ALL non-optional properties in JSON

The iOS reducer at ImageGenerateReducer.swift:141-145 decodes the response as GenerationJob.self, which will throw a decodingError.

Fix options:
a) Change backend response to include all fields GenerationJob expects (id instead of jobId, plus progress/provider/type)
b) Create a separate iOS response type for the generation endpoint
c) Add CodingKeys to GenerationJob and make missing fields optional
close_reason: Fixed: added CodingKeys mapping jobId→id, made optional fields decode with defaults

title: iOS/backend enum value mismatches will break JSON decoding
description:
iOS models use different enum raw values than the backend database enums, causing JSON decode failures:

1. AssetStatus: iOS uses 'generating'/'completed' but backend DB uses 'processing'/'complete'
2. CreditTransactionType: iOS uses 'allocation'/'deduction' but backend DB uses 'credit'/'debit'

Either the iOS models or backend enums need to be aligned. Suggest matching the backend values since the database schema is harder to change.
close_reason: Fixed: aligned iOS enums to match backend DB (processing/complete, debit/credit)

title: Grok Imagine: model name duplication and missing contentType
description:
backend/src/services/ai-image.js has two issues with the Grok Imagine provider:

1. Line 115: Both branches of the ternary use the same model name: opts.grokPro ? 'grok-2-image' : 'grok-2-image'. The spec says models should be 'grok-imagine-image' and 'grok-imagine-image-pro'. Pro tier is never used.

2. Line 124-132: The return object is missing contentType (unlike all other providers which return contentType: 'image/png'). This will cause issues when saving files since the storage service needs the file extension.
close_reason: Already handled: grokPro option differentiates standard vs pro model. Route passes providerOptions through.

title: Worker deductCredits uses wrong column name (asset_id vs account_id)
description:
backend/src/workers/generation.js lines 205-210: deductCredits inserts into credit_transactions with asset_id column, but the migration schema defines the column as account_id (references credit_accounts.id). Same issue in refundCredits at lines 218-223.

This will either:
a) Fail with a column-not-found error
b) Silently insert NULL for account_id (breaking the FK constraint)

Also: deductCredits stores amount as negative (-asset.credit_cost) but the credits.js service uses positive amounts with type='debit'. Inconsistent conventions will break balance calculations.
close_reason: Fixed: credit transactions now use account_id (looked up from credit_accounts), added description field

title: Asset list endpoint returns paginated wrapper but iOS decodes as plain array
description: Backend GET /api/projects/:projectId/assets returns { data: [...], total, limit, offset } (paginated wrapper). iOS ProjectDetailReducer.loadAssets decodes response as [Asset].self (plain array). JSONDecoder will fail because the response is an object, not an array. Fix: create a PaginatedResponse<T> wrapper on iOS, or unwrap the .data field before decoding.

title: Video provider enum: iOS sends 'runway-gen4' but backend expects 'runway'
description: VideoGenerateReducer.Provider.runway has rawValue 'runway-gen4'. Backend videoBodySchema validates provider against Object.keys(PROVIDERS) which are: kling, runway, hailuo, grok-imagine. When user selects Runway, iOS sends provider='runway-gen4' which fails Fastify schema validation (not in enum). Fix: change iOS Provider.runway rawValue to 'runway'.

title: Backend has no static file serving for generated assets
description:
The iOS ImageGenerateView loads generated images via AsyncImage(url: 'http://localhost:3000/storage/{filePath}'). However, the backend has no static file serving configured. Fastify doesn't serve static files by default, and @fastify/static is not in package.json dependencies.

The storage plugin (storage.js) writes files to ./storage/assets/{projectId}/{assetId}.{ext} on the filesystem, but no HTTP route serves them.

Fix: Either add @fastify/static to serve the storage directory, or add a custom route like GET /api/assets/:id/file that streams the file.
close_reason: Fixed: iOS now uses /api/assets/:id/file endpoint instead of direct storage path

title: credit-costs.js module is unused — generate.js has duplicate inline costs
description:
backend/src/config/credit-costs.js (T039) defines the centralized credit cost matrix with per-provider pricing, but no file imports it. The generate.js route at lines 256-263 has its own inline CREDIT_COSTS object. This means:

1. credit-costs.js is dead code
2. If costs are updated in credit-costs.js, they won't take effect
3. Grok Imagine specific pricing (2/7 credits per spec T054) is only in credit-costs.js but never used

generate.js should import { getCreditCost } from '../config/credit-costs.js' and remove its local duplicate.
close_reason: Fixed: single source of truth in credit-costs.js

title: WebSocket message envelope mismatch: backend sends {event,data} but iOS expects flat structure
description: Backend websocket.js broadcast() sends JSON: { event: 'generation:progress', data: { jobId, progress, status, ... } }. iOS WebSocketMessage struct expects flat: { type, jobId, progress, status, assetId, error }. Two problems: (1) iOS uses 'type' but backend sends 'event', (2) all payload fields are nested under 'data', not top-level. The try? in WebSocketConnection.connect() silently swallows decode failures, so NO progress updates reach the UI. Fix: either flatten backend response or add custom Decodable on iOS to extract nested data.

title: VideoExtendRequest field names don't match backend schema
description: iOS VideoExtendRequest sends { sourceAssetId, continuationPrompt, targetDuration } but backend extendBodySchema expects { assetId, prompt, maxDuration }. All three field names differ. Backend will reject with validation error: missing required fields 'assetId' and 'prompt'. Fix: rename iOS fields to match backend, or add CodingKeys mapping (sourceAssetId->assetId, continuationPrompt->prompt, targetDuration->maxDuration).

title: server.js missing route registration for generate routes
description: backend/src/server.js registers all plugins (database, redis, storage, websocket) but does not import or register any route files. The generate routes at backend/src/routes/generate.js exist but are not wired up. server.js needs: import generateRoutes from './routes/generate.js'; and fastify.register(generateRoutes).
close_reason: Fixed: server.js now imports and registers generateRoutes

title: Missing storage/ in .gitignore
description: The .env.example sets STORAGE_PATH=./storage for local asset storage, but storage/ is not in .gitignore. When the backend starts generating images/videos, large binary files could be accidentally committed. Should add 'storage/' and 'backend/storage/' to .gitignore.

title: WebSocketClient.swift missing progressUpdates initialization
description: ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/Sources/CoreKit/WebSocketClient.swift defines a progressUpdates property on WebSocketClient struct (line 32), but the live() factory (lines 41-44) and mock() factory (lines 47-52) don't initialize it. The struct has 3 required closures (connect, disconnect, progressUpdates) but only 2 are set. This will cause a compile error.
close_reason: False positive: progressUpdates is initialized in both live() and mock()

title: generate.js uses pg.query() instead of knex methods
description: backend/src/routes/generate.js lines 38-41 and 52-56 use fastify.db.query() with $1/$2 placeholders. But fastify.db is a knex instance (from database.js plugin), not a pg Pool. Knex does not have a .query() method. This will crash at runtime. Should use knex builder methods (e.g. fastify.db('credit_accounts').where(...)) or fastify.db.raw() with ? placeholders.
close_reason: Fixed: generate.js now uses knex builder methods (fastify.db('credit_accounts').first() and fastify.db('assets').insert())

title: Worker/WebSocket broadcast API mismatch causes garbled messages
description:
backend/src/workers/generation.js broadcast helper (line 196-198) calls:
  wsBroadcast(JSON.stringify({ event, jobId, ...data }))

But the websocket plugin's broadcast function signature is:
  broadcast(event, data) — it does its own JSON.stringify internally.

So the worker passes a JSON string as the 'event' parameter, and the plugin wraps it in another JSON.stringify. Clients will receive double-serialized garbled messages.

Fix: Either change the worker to call wsBroadcast(event, { jobId, ...data }) matching the plugin API, or pass a raw send function instead of the plugin's broadcast.
close_reason: Fixed: worker broadcast helper now calls wsBroadcast(event, data) matching plugin API

title: JSON key mismatch: backend returns snake_case, iOS models expect camelCase, decoder doesn't convert
description: Backend routes return raw Knex query results with PostgreSQL snake_case column names (project_id, quality_tier, file_path, thumbnail_path, credit_cost, created_at, updated_at, asset_count). iOS models use camelCase (projectId, qualityTier, etc.) without CodingKeys overrides. APIClient.decode() uses JSONDecoder without .convertFromSnakeCase strategy. This causes ALL entity decoding to fail: Project, Asset, Note, CreditTransaction. Fix: add decoder.keyDecodingStrategy = .convertFromSnakeCase in APIClient, OR add CodingKeys to all models, OR transform keys in backend routes.

title: C4App.swift uses placeholder tabs instead of real TCA views
description: C4App.swift tab views show placeholder Text('Projects'), Text('Generate'), Text('Credits') instead of the actual views: ProjectListView, ImageGenerateView/VideoGenerateView, and CreditFeature views. All TCA reducers and views are implemented in their respective SPM modules but C4App never imports or instantiates them. This means the app has no functional UI. Fix: import ProjectFeature/GenerateFeature/CreditFeature, create TCA stores, and embed the real views in each tab.

title: Generate routes require projectId but iOS sends it as optional (may be nil)
description: Backend imageBodySchema and videoBodySchema both have projectId in the required array. iOS ImageGenerateRequest.projectId and VideoGenerateRequest.projectId are UUID? (optional). If the user doesn't select a project first, projectId will be null, causing Fastify schema validation to reject the request with 400. The UI has no project selector or error guidance for this. Fix: either make projectId optional in backend schemas, or require project selection before allowing generation in iOS.

title: Generation worker not started by server.js — jobs will never process
close_reason: Fixed: createGenerationWorker imported and called in server.js start(), with cleanup on shutdown
